data.description:
## Incident Overview

- **Scenario Type:** Test reliability failure (CI-only)

#### Expected Behavior
test-merge-fallback-exists-in-lib should PASS because ticket-lib.sh contains the merge fallback pattern.

#### Actual Behavior
In CI (Linux, bash, set -uo pipefail), the test fails with 'FAIL: merge fallback NOT found in _push_tickets_branch' and 'echo: write error: Broken pipe'.

Root cause: `echo "$lib_content" | grep -qE '...'` pattern. grep -q exits immediately after finding a match, closing the pipe. The bash built-in echo then gets SIGPIPE when writing the rest of $lib_content into the closed pipe. With set -uo pipefail, the pipeline exit code is the first non-zero exit (SIGPIPE=141 from echo), so the if condition evaluates to false even though grep found the match.

Fix: Replace `echo "$lib_content" | grep -qE '...' "$TICKET_LIB"` with `grep -qE '...' "$TICKET_LIB"` — grep the file directly, avoiding the echo|grep-q pipe pattern entirely.

CI evidence: https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/actions/runs/24933519687
Log line: test-ticket-push-rebase-conflict.sh: line 56: echo: write error: Broken pipe
data.title: [test-ticket-push-rebase-conflict]: echo|grep-q+pipefail SIGPIPE -> merge fallback test false-negative in CI
